events.image_prompt: Silent tram interior, grey dystopian Brno 2157, emotionless passengers, cold metallic lighting, oppressive atmosphere
events.text:
The tram arrives without announcement, without friction, without the courtesy of sound. It simply aligns itself with the platform as if it had always been there and you merely noticed it late. The doors separate with surgical precision. No hiss. No mechanical fatigue.

You step inside with the others. No one pushes. No one hesitates. The spacing between bodies is too even to be coincidence, too natural to be enforced. You sit. The seat is cold but not uncomfortable. It feels designed for compliance.

Outside, Brno slides past in flattened tones of grey and diluted copper. Towers like ledgers. Streets like ruled paper. Everything aligned. Everything accounted for.

Then something shifts.

It is small.
It is obscene.

The tram occupies two positions at once.

One version slightly ahead along the rail.
One slightly behind.

For less than a heartbeat you perceive both.
Two realities sharing the same space like overlapping reflections in imperfect glass.

Then the world tightens.
The rear version vanishes.
The forward version remains.

No one reacts.
No breath sharpens.
No eyes widen.

The city continues, obedient.

choices.text: || It was a trick of perception. Fatigue distorts edges. You should not look for patterns where none exist.
|| If the tram split, something chose which version remained. The question is not what you saw, but who decided.
|| Observe the passengers carefully. If reality shifted, someone must have felt it.

events.image_prompt: Muted tram interior, passengers emotionless, dim grey lighting, oppressive stillness
events.text:
You lower your gaze. Concrete pillars pass outside at predictable intervals. Predictability is a comfort. Predictability is proof.

Your pulse slows deliberately, as if responding to instruction.

The mind is unreliable. It fills silence with invention. You know this. You work with projections, probabilities, statistical anomalies. A momentary overlap of perception is nothing more than neural misfire.

And yet.

You become aware of a faint vibration beneath your feet. Not mechanical. Not rhythmic.

Corrective.

The tram glides onward. The sensation fades the moment you attempt to isolate it.

The world rewards your refusal to question it.

For now.

choices.text: Maintain composure. Routine is stability. Stability is survival.
Even if it was nothing, the body reacted first. Trust the body over the explanation.

events.image_prompt: Dystopian Brno street from tram window, slight visual distortion in air, muted brutalist city
events.text:
You look through the window again, deliberately this time.

A pedestrian approaches a crossing. A car moves too fast. The geometry of impact assembles in your mind with brutal clarity.

You see the collision.
You see her body thrown forward.
You see the arc of blood against compliant asphalt.
You see the driver’s face collapse inward.

The future exists. Fully formed.

Then the woman hesitates.
Half a step less than she intended.
The car passes cleanly.

The violent future dissolves without residue.

Your throat tightens.

You did not imagine the accident.
You witnessed a branch that did not survive.

choices.text: This is probability. The mind simulates outcomes constantly. Nothing supernatural occurred.
If branches are visible, they are being trimmed. You need to know who holds the shears.

events.image_prompt: Close-up of passengers in tram, emotionless faces, faint distortion in reflection
events.text:
You study the passengers one by one.

A woman with tired eyes. A man in a gray coat. A student holding a data-slate. Their breathing is synchronized more closely than chance allows.

The man in the gray coat blinks.
Too late.
As if his eyelids received the instruction after the correction completed.

For a fraction of a second, your reflection in the window lags behind your movement.
You turn your head.
The reflection turns after you.

The delay is microscopic.
It is devastating.

The man in gray meets your gaze.
He does not look surprised.
He looks calibrated.

choices.text: Look away. Survival depends on not being noticed.
Hold his gaze. If there is awareness, you will force it into the open.

events.image_prompt: Massive brutalist government interior, high ceilings, grey marble, oppressive symmetry
events.text:
The Department building absorbs you without resistance.

No ornamentation.
No excess geometry.
Only structure.

Your badge unlocks the entrance before you consciously present it.

Inside, the air feels heavier than outside. Not humid. Not warm.
Dense.

As if more reality is packed into each cubic meter.

Workstations extend in ordered rows. Each screen activates as its assigned employee approaches. No commands are spoken.

Your terminal illuminates.

Trajectory Adjustment Review – Subject B-741.

Probability branches unfold across the display like vascular systems.
Red lines thicken toward a single outcome labeled:

Deviation Risk: Escalating.

Below it:
Minor Neural Recadrement Required.

The word “minor” feels administrative.
Not descriptive.

choices.text: Approve the correction. It is procedural. You are not here to question trajectory mathematics.
Expand the full branch analysis. If a life bends, you should see the direction.
Hesitate. Observe the room first. See if anything shifts when you delay.
